benign_tumor: The 'malignant' class in the BUSI dataset is primarily characterized by irregular, often angular or spiculated shapes and ill-defined or microlobulated margins.
Malignant tumors often have a non-parallel orientation (taller-than-wide) and may present with posterior acoustic shadowing.
Malignant lesions are invasive and have irregular, poorly defined borders, unlike benign tumors.
Malignant tumors are distinguished from benign by their aggressive features and from normal by the presence of a suspicious mass.
Malignant tumors often show increased vascularity and local invasion, which are not seen in benign or normal scans.
malignant_tumor: The 'malignant' class in the BUSI dataset is primarily characterized by irregular, often angular or spiculated shapes and ill-defined or microlobulated margins.
Malignant tumors often have a non-parallel orientation (taller-than-wide) and may present with posterior acoustic shadowing.
Malignant lesions are invasive and have irregular, poorly defined borders, unlike benign tumors.
Malignant tumors are distinguished from benign by their aggressive features and from normal by the presence of a suspicious mass.
Malignant tumors often show increased vascularity and local invasion, which are not seen in benign or normal scans.
normal_scan: The 'normal' class in the BUSI dataset is primarily distinguished by the absence of discrete lesions or masses, presenting a homogeneous and unremarkable parenchymal echotexture.
Unlike benign or malignant tumors, normal scans show no abnormal growths or suspicious features.
Normal breast ultrasound images lack any pathological structures, which are present in both benign and malignant cases.
The tissue in normal scans is uniform, without the irregularities seen in tumor images.
Normal scans are differentiated by the complete absence of masses, unlike the other classes.
